title: Crispy French Mushroom Croquettes
description: Salty, chewy, energizing French-inspired croquettes made with chestnut mushrooms and a crisp breadcrumb coating; perfect for a Jain-friendly snack or side.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium heat. Add sliced chestnut mushrooms and sauté until softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add fresh thyme and parsley to the skillet. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant, then remove from heat.
Transfer the mushroom mixture to a bowl and let it cool slightly. Stir in sea salt and black pepper to taste.
In a small saucepan, whisk chickpea flour with water until smooth. Heat gently, stirring, until the mixture thickens into a pliable batter, about 2 minutes. Let it cool.
Fold the cooled mushroom mixture into the chickpea batter until well combined. If the mixture feels too liquid, add a little more chickpea flour or water to reach a thick, dough‑like consistency.
Optional: stir in nutritional yeast for added umami and a subtle cheesy note.
Divide the mixture into 12 equal portions and shape each into a cylinder or oval croquette.
Place the panko breadcrumbs in a shallow dish. Roll each croquette in breadcrumbs, pressing gently to coat evenly.
Preheat the air fryer to 200 °C (400 °F). Lightly spray the basket with cooking spray and arrange the croquettes in a single layer, leaving a small gap between them.
Air fry the croquettes for 12–15 minutes, turning halfway through, until golden and crisp. Serve immediately, optionally with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.
